_meta.schema_purpose: Canonical company profile schema for opportunity evaluation. Populated by research agent. Consumed by scoring agent + downstream agents (CV, cover letter, interview prep).
changelog: v0.2 (2026-04-21): Added 360_synthesis block (hybrid C-model with rubric version tag), name_confusion_check, gate_needs_judgment_call array, parallel_business_flag in leadership, structured war_position per leader. Driven by MEDvidi evaluation.
v0.1: Initial schema covering snapshot, business_model, strategy, leadership, market_view, insider_signal, hiring_signal, operator_fit, risks.
_meta.consumption_model: Hybrid (Option C from Layer 1 decision 2026-04-21). Research agent populates facts + signals. Scoring agent populates 360_synthesis. Downstream agents read profile. If synthesis_rubric_version != current_rubric_version, downstream agents trigger re-synthesis before proceeding.

instance_meta.confidence_notes: string — flag what relies on self-reported vs verified data, what's missing, known gotchas (e.g. name confusion)

name_confusion_check._purpose: Proactively surface similarly-named companies that could contaminate research. Added v0.2 after MEDvidi/MEDVi confusion during that evaluation. Research agent MUST populate this field — even a null explicit entry is a signal the agent looked.

similar_names_found.name: string — the lookalike
similar_names_found.distinguishing_facts: string — what separates this entity from the target
similar_names_found.notes: string — e.g. 'shares TCPA class-action plaintiffs bar', 'similar revenue model', 'frequently confused in press coverage'

name_confusion_check.none_found: boolean — true if agent actively searched and found no confusable lookalikes

snapshot.profitability_claim: string — note if self-reported vs verified

business_model.structure: string — legal/operational structure (MSO/PC, franchise, platform, etc.)

leadership._note: Each leader entry now carries war_position (if RU/BY origin) and parallel_business_flag. See field definitions below.

_leader_entry_schema.name: string
_leader_entry_schema.title: string
_leader_entry_schema.origin: string — country of origin, relevant for carve-out gates
_leader_entry_schema.background: string — prior roles, exits, track record
_leader_entry_schema.public_voice_signal: string — how they present publicly; relevant for manager-quality pre-signal

war_position._purpose: Added v0.2. Structured per-leader field for RU/BY-origin founders. Per rubric v0.2, RU and BY origin are scored asymmetrically (see rubric.asymmetric_war_position_logic).
war_position.applies: boolean — true only if origin is RU or BY; else null/false and all sub-fields are N/A
war_position.value: explicit_anti_war | explicit_pro_war | accommodative | silent | not_researched | null
war_position.evidence: string — source and quote if explicit; 'no public statements found in [search scope]' if silent
war_position.research_scope: string — what the agent actually searched (e.g. 'LinkedIn + web + Russian-language sources + published interviews')
war_position.confidence: high | medium | low — low if search scope was limited or language-barriered

parallel_business_flag._purpose: Added v0.2. Flags when a leader is actively operating another business alongside their role at the target company. Particularly material when the parallel business is in a carve-out jurisdiction (RU/BY).

businesses.name: string
businesses.jurisdiction: string — country where the parallel business operates
businesses.overlap_risk: string — is there engineering/data/PHI overlap between the parallel business and the target company?
businesses.carve_out_escalation: boolean — true if jurisdiction is in RU/BY or other carve-out region

_leader_entry_schema.concentration_risk_notes: string — e.g. 'CEO personally drives public narrative, unclear how much is delegated'

dissonance_flags: string — where JD claims don't match public voice

hiring_signal.hand_off_test_status: string — 'unknown — test in call' or actual answer if surfaced

gate_needs_judgment_call._purpose: Added v0.2. Explicit array of gates that cannot be auto-resolved from public research. Scoring agent and downstream agents check this array before proceeding. If non-empty, downstream work is blocked pending human resolution OR explicit override.
gate_needs_judgment_call.blocked: boolean — true if any gate_id in the array is unresolved

items.gate_id: string — matches a gate id from rubric
items.reason: string — why public research couldn't resolve it
recommended_probes: string — specific questions to ask in first call to resolve

360_synthesis._purpose: Added v0.2. The scoring agent's integrated read — stance + reasoning — based on everything above. Per hybrid C-model: cached here, consumed by downstream agents, re-generated when rubric version changes or manual trigger fires.
360_synthesis.synthesis_rubric_version: string — rubric version this synthesis was generated against. If != current rubric version, downstream agents must trigger re-synthesis.
360_synthesis.stance_one_line: string — single sentence summary of the recommendation
360_synthesis.stance_reasoning: string — 2-4 paragraphs on why. Integrates hard gates, scored criteria, override rules, judgment-call gates. Reads like the 'Operator fit — my read' section of a human-readable 360.
override_rules_invoked: string — which override applied and why
first_call_probes: string — prioritized list of questions for first conversation. Includes gate-resolution probes (from gate_needs_judgment_call) plus scored-criteria probes (manager-quality pressure tests, hand-off test, etc.)
360_synthesis.recommended_next_action: string — concrete, e.g. 'apply cold', 'warm intro first', 'resolve gate X before engagement', 'pass'

source_index._format: Keys are short IDs (S1, S2, ...). Values are URLs. All factual claims in the profile should cite source IDs.
